items.question: Do I need to have read the book myself first?
items.answer: Not at all. The teacher guide walks you through every discussion with scripted facilitation questions, context notes, and week-by-week structure. You can teach this confidently even if it's your first time reading the text alongside your student.

items.question: Is this curriculum secular or faith-based?
items.answer: It is faith-friendly. God is not left out when He is genuinely present in the literature or the ideas — and in works like Macbeth and Wuthering Heights, questions of moral order and the soul are impossible to ignore. But this is not a devotional curriculum. Secular families use it comfortably alongside families of faith.

items.question: What is the difference between the Homeschool and Co-op versions?
items.answer: The Homeschool version is designed for a parent teaching one or a few students at home — it moves at your own pace with a parent guide and flexible structure. The Co-op version includes a full teacher guide with scripted discussion, classroom-ready handouts, and materials designed for weekly 90-minute sessions with a group.

items.question: What grade level is this really for?
items.answer: The units are designed for high school, grades 9–12. That said, a strong 8th grader can absolutely handle the material, and the honors track extends the challenge for advanced students. The DELIGHT framework scales well — it meets students where they are.

items.question: Can I use this without a co-op?
items.answer: Absolutely. The homeschool version is built for exactly that. Many families use it as a one-on-one literature course at home, working through the narrations and discussions together as a family read-aloud or with an independent high schooler.

items.question: Do I need any other curriculum alongside this?
items.answer: Delight & Savor is a complete literature and language arts course — reading, analysis, writing, and grammar are all woven together through the unit. You will need a copy of the novel or play itself. Some families add a separate composition program; others find this course covers their writing goals fully.
